title: Flatten nested lists
description: Write flatten(nested) that flattens arbitrarily nested lists into a single flat list. Non-list elements (including strings) are kept as-is.
constraints: Only list instances are flattened — strings and tuples are atoms.
Handle arbitrary nesting depth (within recursion limits).
edge_cases: strings inside lists must NOT be exploded into characters
properties: nested = [nested, a] if rng.random() < 0.5 else nested + [a]
assert fn(nested) == [a for a in atoms]
